title: Lord, I Lift My Soul to Thee

verses.text: Lord, I lift my soul to thee, O my God, I trust thy might; let not foes exult o'er me, shame me not before their sight. The Psalter, 1912
Yea, may none be put to shame, None who wait for thee to bless; But dishonored be their name Who without a cause transgress. The Psalter, 1912
Lord, to me thy ways make known, guide in truth and teach thou me; thou my Savior art alone, all the day I wait for thee. The Psalter, 1912
Lord, remember in thy love all thy mercies manifold, tender mercies from above, changeless from the days of old. The Psalter, 1912
Sins of youth remember not. Nor my trespasses record; Let not mercy be forgot, For thy goodness’ sake, O Lord. The Psalter, 1912
Just and good the Lord abides, he his way will sinners show; he the meek in justice guides, making them his way to know. The Psalter, 1912
Grace and truth shall mark the way where the Lord his own will lead, if his word they still obey and his testimonies heed. The Psalter, 1912
For Thy name’s sake hear thou me; For thy mercy, Lord I wait; Pardon my iniquity, For my sin is very great. The Psalter, 1912 The Psalter, 1912
He who walks in godly fear In the path of truth shall go Peace shall be his portion here, And his sons all good shall know
